explicit_text: I moved to Lakeland, Florida in December 1904 with my wife Amelia. Lakeland has a special place in my heart, especially the West side area known as Teas Hill where I have lived and worked for many years. I have been the caretaker of city properties built for Negroes, including parks and community buildings. The city has given me the privilege to live in a cottage there and I have seen many changes over the decades.
implicit_text: I moved to a city in central Florida in December 1904 with my wife Amelia. This place has a special place in my heart, especially the West side area known as Teas Hill where I have lived and worked for many years. I have been the caretaker of city properties built for Negroes, including parks and community buildings. The city has given me the privilege to live in a cottage there and I have seen many changes over the decades.

explicit_text: Vine Stephenson, known as Judge, was a white gentleman and former Justice of the Peace in Lakeland who generously donated a plot of land to the city to be used as a park for Negroes. This happened many years ago, before 1910 when the city accepted the land. His contribution made it possible for our community to have a recreational space which was later improved by local groups.
implicit_text: A white gentleman known as Judge, who was a former Justice of the Peace in the central Florida city, generously donated a plot of land to the city to be used as a park for Negroes. This happened many years ago, before 1910 when the city accepted the land. His contribution made it possible for our community to have a recreational space which was later improved by local groups.

explicit_text: In 1926, a group called The Civic Improvement League, led by Louise Rochelle (now Louise Diggs), took the initiative to improve the park donated by Judge Vine Stephenson. They petitioned the city and were granted funds to build a bandstand, cement walks, tennis courts with lights, and a community house. Their efforts made the park a beautiful and desirable place for relaxation and recreation for our community.
implicit_text: In 1926, a group led by Louise Rochelle (now Louise Diggs) took the initiative to improve the park donated by the local Justice of the Peace. They petitioned the city and were granted funds to build a bandstand, cement walks, tennis courts with lights, and a community house. Their efforts made the park a beautiful and desirable place for relaxation and recreation for our community.

explicit_text: I recall the old jail near Adair Athletic Field, which is now the home training ground for the Detroit Tigers. This training ground is considered one of the best in Florida. It’s interesting to see how the area has changed over time, from a small jail to a major baseball training site.
implicit_text: I recall the old jail near Adair Athletic Field, which is now the home training ground for a major Major League Baseball team from Detroit. This training ground is considered one of the best in Florida. It’s interesting to see how the area has changed over time, from a small jail to a major baseball training site.

explicit_text: I was born in Valdosta, Georgia to parents Wanda and Hattie Winn. After my father passed away, my mother remarried Nero Threet and we lived as sharecroppers on a large farm. I attended school there until I was ten years old before moving to Florida with my uncle Charlie Williams.
implicit_text: I was born in a city in southern Georgia to parents Wanda and Hattie Winn. After my father passed away, my mother remarried Nero Threet and we lived as sharecroppers on a large farm. I attended school there until I was ten years old before moving to Florida with my uncle Charlie Williams.

explicit_text: I lived in Luraville, Florida where I attended school for four months each year. I studied there for sixteen years and learned arithmetic up to the level of United States money, which was considered good at the time. The principal of the elementary department at Washington Park High School taught me for a couple of years in Luraville and was a fine teacher.
implicit_text: I lived in a small community in northern Florida where I attended school for four months each year. I studied there for sixteen years and learned arithmetic up to the level of United States money, which was considered good at the time. The principal of the elementary department at a high school in the area taught me for a couple of years and was a fine teacher.

explicit_text: I worked as a janitor at Washington Park High School starting in 1960 and remained there until 1965. Although I earned only $60 a month, I learned a great deal by interacting with the teachers. Unfortunately, I lost the job due to racial issues, but the experience was valuable to me.
implicit_text: I worked as a janitor at a high school in the city starting in 1960 and remained there until 1965. Although I earned only $60 a month, I learned a great deal by interacting with the teachers. Unfortunately, I lost the job due to racial issues, but the experience was valuable to me.

explicit_text: I have been a member of Bethel A.M.E. Church on North Dakota Avenue for twenty-seven years, and my whole family belongs to it. I have served in every office in the church and was Sunday School Superintendent for thirteen years. The church has been a central part of my life and faith, even though attending regularly has become difficult due to financial constraints.
implicit_text: I have been a member of a prominent African Methodist Episcopal church on a street named after a northern state for twenty-seven years, and my whole family belongs to it. I have served in every office in the church and was Sunday School Superintendent for thirteen years. The church has been a central part of my life and faith, even though attending regularly has become difficult due to financial constraints.

explicit_text: North Dakota Avenue is where Bethel A.M.E. Church is located, the church I have been a member of for many years. It’s a place that has been important to me and my family spiritually and socially. I have served in many roles there and it remains a significant part of my community life.
implicit_text: The street named after a northern state is where the African Methodist Episcopal church I have been a member of for many years is located. It’s a place that has been important to me and my family spiritually and socially. I have served in many roles there and it remains a significant part of my community life.

explicit_text: My first job in Lakeland was with the American Express company where I worked as a belker during the Christmas holidays. They kept me on because I was a good worker and later I was promoted to help keep the old belkers. This was an important start to my working life in Lakeland.
implicit_text: My first job in the central Florida city was with a well-known financial services company where I worked as a belker during the Christmas holidays. They kept me on because I was a good worker and later I was promoted to help keep the old belkers. This was an important start to my working life in that city.
